zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd於1332年出生在威爾士布雷康史爾。他和Catrin ferch William至少有6個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於大約1398年在威爾士享年67歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd于1332年出生在威尔士布雷康史爾。他和Catrin ferch William至少有6个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于大约1398年在威尔士享年67岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾波萊斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Dafydd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of David . In widespread use during the Middle Ages, it was later largely replaced by the English form, but since the late 19th century it has come into its own again.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd was born in 1332, in Breconshire, Wales. He had at least 6 sons and 2 daughters with Catrin ferch William. He died about 1398, in Wales, at the age of 67, and was buried in Polesworth, Warwickshire, England.

fr.of,Brecon,Breconshire,Wales: Brecon, Breconshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd est né en 1332, à Breconshire, Galles. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles avec Catrin ferch William. Il est décédé environ 1398, à Galles, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Polesworth, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd wurde 1332 in Breconshire, Wales geboren. Er hatte mit Catrin ferch William mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1398 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Wales und wurde in Polesworth, Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd è nato il nel 1332 nel Breconshire, Galles. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie con Catrin ferch William. Morì circa 1398, nel Galles, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Polesworth, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwydは1332年にウェールズブレコンシュアイアで生まれた。彼はCatrin ferch Williamとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1398年にウェールズで67歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアポレスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd(은)는 1332년 웨일스 브레콘실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Catrin ferch William(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1398년 웨일스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 포레스워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd nasceu em 1332, em Breconshire, Gales. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas com Catrin ferch William. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1398, em Gales, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Polesworth, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd родился в 1332 г. в местности брекуншир, Уэльс. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери от Catrin ferch William. Он умер около 1398 г. в местности Уэльс в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности полесворт, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Ap Dafydd Llwyd nació en 1332, en Breconshire, Gales. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas con Catrin ferch William. Murió aproximadamente 1398, en Gales, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Polesworth, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
